validation.approved_by: DELIVER orchestrator (chisel worker) — decomposition matches distill Phase 04 scope + ADR-026 §Decision outcome item 3 (lake quote_ident hygiene) + MR-4 row. Single-step phase: hygiene fix at known interpolation sites with characterization tests authored before the change per CLAUDE.md brownfield discipline.

roadmap.scope_note: Phase 04 only (MR-4). Phases 01 + 02 + 03 already on main (azurite Phase 03 record preserved at deliver/roadmap-phase-03.json and deliver/wave-decisions-phase-03.md). Phase 05 ships on a later crew clone.

phases.name: MR-4 lake-repo quote_ident hygiene (closes Gap 3)
phases.description: Apply quote_ident at lake-repo column-name interpolation sites between lines 214-277 of repository.py; add characterization tests covering edge characters per Feathers brownfield discipline.

steps.name: Apply quote_ident at lake repository column interpolation sites
steps.scenario: MR-4 hygiene contract: all column-name interpolation in BaseLakeRepository's read paths uses quote_ident() and survives edge characters in column names
steps.scenario_name: Lake repository quotes column identifiers correctly across edge characters
criteria: All column-name interpolation in backend/app/repositories/lake/repository.py between lines 214 and 277 uses quote_ident() (no raw f-string interpolation of column names)
Characterization tests cover embedded double-quotes, embedded delimiters, mixed-case identifiers, and keyword-like names for get_parquet_column_type
Walking-skeleton, milestone-1, and milestone-2 acceptance scenarios remain GREEN after the hygiene fix
Backend gate ./tools/test/test.sh --backend passes; existing lake repository tests still green
Step-ID trailer present on the commit
steps.implementation_notes: READ FIRST: backend/app/repositories/lake/repository.py (lines 200-360 — preview / count / typeof / cleaning); backend/app/utils/sql_safety.py (quote_ident, quote_literal); backend/app/use_cases/query_engine/sync_processor.py (the reference style — see lines 59-60, 89-90, 118-119 for how quote_ident is applied around DDL identifiers). CURRENT STATE: line 247 of repository.py emits `f"SELECT typeof(\"{column}\") AS col_type FROM read_parquet('{s3_path}') LIMIT 1"` — raw `\"{column}\"` interpolation. The other call sites between 214 and 277 either (a) have no column interpolation (the count query at line 214 only takes s3_path) or (b) already use `col = quote_ident(target_column)` via the local binding at line 260 inside preview_cleaning_operation. The fix is therefore narrow: switch line 247 to `quote_ident(column)`. CHARACTERIZATION TESTS BEFORE CHANGE (Feathers brownfield): pin the asyncpg `fetchrow` call argument via a mock on the connection (mocks at the asyncpg PORT BOUNDARY per CLAUDE.md), assert the SQL string the production code passes for a normal column name (e.g. `region`) before the fix, then make the fix, then assert the new SQL is `SELECT typeof("region") AS col_type FROM read_parquet('{s3_path}') LIMIT 1` — byte-identical to the legacy raw-escaped form for the regular-identifier case. EDGE CHARACTER TESTS (these are what the hygiene fix gains): for a column named `col"with"quote`, the fix produces `"col""with""quote"` (quote_ident doubles embedded quotes); for `col,region` the SQL is `"col,region"` (delimiter passes through unchanged inside the quoted identifier); for `MyCol` the SQL is `"MyCol"` (case preserved); for `select` (SQL keyword) the SQL is `"select"` (quoting prevents reserved-word collision). PROHIBITED: no .replace('\"','\"\"') ad-hoc escaping outside quote_ident; no string concatenation of column names in the SQL emission path. WIRE TESTS via a minimal stub for the pool/connection: build a small AsyncMock that captures the SQL passed to `conn.fetchrow` and returns a stub row with `col_type` keyed value. The test_lake_repository.py file is new — create it under backend/tests/repositories/ (existing test layout puts repository unit tests as flat files there, not under a lake/ subdirectory). DESIGN BOUNDARY: do not refactor adjacent methods (read_parquet_preview, get_parquet_row_count, preview_cleaning_operation); they are already either column-free or already using quote_ident via the local `col` binding. Scope is exactly the one f-string at line 247 plus the new test file.

exit_criteria_phase_04: Grep `f"[^"]*\\"\\{` against backend/app/repositories/lake/repository.py between lines 200-300 returns no matches (no raw double-quote interpolation of column names)
backend/tests/repositories/test_lake_repository.py exists with characterization tests for the four edge-character classes (embedded double-quotes, embedded delimiters, mixed case, keyword-like)
Reviewer approves: hygiene fix matches the sync_processor.py style (quote_ident at the f-string interpolation point, no ad-hoc escaping)
manual_review_gate_after_phase_04: MR-4 hygiene fix lands at backend/app/repositories/lake/repository.py:247 (the typeof query). All other column-name interpolation in the 214-277 range was already routed through quote_ident via the local `col = quote_ident(target_column)` binding in preview_cleaning_operation. Characterization tests pin the post-fix shape against four edge-character classes. Per ADR-026 §Consequences → Positive → Closes Gap 3.
